definition: The arising or originating condition of becoming within dependent-arising analysis.
notes: Use this record when the origin-side compound is named compactly as bhava-samudaya rather than quoted as a fuller origin-line sentence. Keep the compound aligned with the headword defaults for bhava and samudaya, and do not drift from becoming to being or existence by habit inside the standard chain.

example_phrases.notes: Compact origin-side compound in the dependent-arising family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for the compact named bhava-samudaya compound inside the origin-side dependent-arising family; prefer fuller origin-line wording when the canonical sentence itself is present.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not rotate the compound to being or existence by habit when the dependent-arising frame is still governing the passage.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents origin-side bhava language from drifting away from the repository's becoming default.
